programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The first instalment of a two-part crossover with NCIS: New Orleans sees the Washington, DC team co-operating with their colleagues from the Big Easy to investigate a spate of lethal poisonings on board a New Orleans-to-DC flight. All the evidence seems to point to missing chef Luca, who happens to be Abby's brother, so the two investigative services join forces to locate him, and find out why a private tech firm seems to have been targetted by the killer. Naval crime drama, guest starring Scott Bakula

Light-hearted drama, starring Richard Briers and Susan Hampshire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New recruit Sergeant Callum Stone (Sam Callis) makes an instant impact at Sun Hill by subduing a violent drunk in the station, but his maverick methods instantly alarm Dan and raise Smithy's hackles. Terry traces a series of threatening calls made to elderly women back to a single suspect, a supervisor at a kitchen fitting company, who is less than pleased to be arrested in a lift stalled between floors. Michael Angelis guest stars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Joseph reacts with horror when he hears that Faye has been charged with Archie's murder, while Thandie notices the effect the news has on Lauren and pleads with her to come clean about her part in the tragic events. Oliver's determination to see a difficult case through to the end impresses Jac, and after the excitement of a difficult operation the pair get closer, leaving Penny to attend the New Year's Eve party alone. Donna and Maria fail in their plan to get a kiss at the do, but unknown to them it becomes clear that one of the pair has caught Nicky's eye

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Abs continues to give Nina the cold shoulder at work and loses his cool when Ellen tries to worm her way back into his good books. Meanwhile, Tess gives Comfort the doctor's verdict on the cause of her baby's death as she prepares to take compassionate leave. Woody and Luke try a spot of matchmaking for Charlie and Harry, and Bex thinks she's found 'the one' after a night with Pete

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following a report of vandalism on a church, Terry finds himself investigating a case of assisted suicide in which conflicting evidence from the grieving family members suggests someone has something to hide. Meanwhile, Beth and Roger are called to a pet shop where the owner has been tied up and several dogs let loose, but it's Sun Hill's own bloodhound Callum Stone who realises there is something more sinister than youthful high-jinks going on

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sarah's shoplifting goes too far. Tony and Dan decide to go big time, but will they be safe from Ray's heavies? Joe goes missing again and Lorraine is frantic with worry

programme.desc: The police arrive at the Butchers' house, David worries about Joe, and Bianca convinces Carol to be a model

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A confectioner's beloved cat falls victim to a mystery illness that defies all James's attempts to find a cure. Rural drama, with Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and Geoffrey Bayldon

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey injures her back, but soon recovers when DeVere invites her on a skiing trip. However, when the entrepreneur gives her advance lessons on the estate, he manages to end up the invalid. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The management crack down on the staff's long coffee breaks, and introduce a check-in book for them to sign. Mrs Slocombe makes a stand against the time-and-motion men - and Mr Mash calls the union out in sympathy. Department-store comedy, starring John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Larry Martyn

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo enlists his friends in a scheme to help Toby win back his ex-wife - but finds she only has eyes for Morton. Meanwhile, Howard tries to keep Pearl from interfering with his bike ride with Marina, and Cooper and Walsh test a new piece of in-car technology. Long-running rural comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Trevor Bannister, Peter Sallis and Burt Kwouk

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Pearl throws Howard out of the house, prompting him to declare he has sworn off women for good - but Hobbo and friends take it upon themselves to get him to talk to Marina first. Meanwhile, Cooper and Walsh have their car valeted after receiving complaints from the sergeant, Glenda tries to whip herself and Barry into shape in time for Gloria's wedding, and Hobbo finds it difficult to avoid Miss Davenport's attentions at the library

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The powers-that-be pile the pressure on Deed to acquit a violent MI5 informer who may be able to infiltrate the Real IRA, leading him to cross swords with the Lord Chancellor's department. To complicate matters, a furious Sir Ian Rochester suspects his wife may be having an affair with him and resolves to bring the maverick into line

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reopens the investigation into an actress's disappearance when clips of her being assaulted in a 1990 movie are posted on the internet. Director Don Maddox features in the footage, but when questioned, he appears to have problems distinguishing fact from fiction. A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. Imogen Stubbs and Sandra Dickinson also guest star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: The Cold Case squad tries to solve a murder dating back to the Second World War. In 1948, a former conscientious objector was found killed with a nine-inch nail driven into his head. New evidence suggests he may have been the victim of a state-sponsored execution, and there may be other crimes to be uncovered. Feature-length episode, starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A woman is robbed of her winnings on the way home from a casino, and suspicion soon falls on the barman who called her a taxi - which was driven by his friend. programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.desc: The coppers investigate the discovery of a body unearthed during renovations at a new university, and are left puzzling over blackmail, mistaken identity and possible suicide. Feature-length detective drama, with Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan and Prunella Scales

Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook

programme.desc: An abandoned car belonging to a former Home Secretary is discovered in a Belfast garage, containing a skeleton and an unexploded bomb. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The Sun Hill cops spring into action when an elderly Alzheimer's sufferer goes missing along with her five-month-old great-granddaughter. Meanwhile, Phil Hunter visits the casino and is surprised when the owner Louis Dreyfuss once again offers him the chance to become his private detective - and before long he finds himself trailing the man's wife to determine if she's having an affair. Jean Boht and Michael Brandon guest star

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's the culmination of Bianca's enthusiasm for selling clothes when she holds a fashion show in the Square, with Ricky and Tiffany as models

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Blossom gives Alan some good advice while Carol gets the bride ready for the big day. Pauline reveals a secret to Willy, and Mark has an important meeting to attend

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The company is sent to New Zealand for a training exercise, where Paddy and Dave take a new recruit under their wing. Guest starring Lena Headey

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe investigates a psychic's claims to have received a sack of poison-pen letters, and concludes her apparent plight is all a cheap publicity stunt - until he uncovers a possible link between the hate mail and a local girl's death. Offbeat crime drama, starring Richard Griffiths, Maggie Steed and Selina Cadell

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tragedy mars a birthday celebration, while a late-night drink leaves James and Siegfried facing the long arm of the law. Rural drama, guest starring Dorothy Tutin, with Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey leads locals in a rally against the proposed closure of the village's railway station, while DeVere harbours plans to build a new supermarket on the site. Comedy, guest starring Richard Thorp

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Lucas decides he's been to his granny's funeral once too often, and instead feigns illness so he can take the day off to visit his girlfriend. Department store comedy, with Mollie Sugden, Nicholas Smith and Trevor Bannister

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wondering if Pearl still loves him, Howard asks Hobbo to give her a peck on the cheek from him - only to become even more despondent when he sees his wife deliver a passionate kiss. Meanwhile, Glenda sends Barry off to paint a landscape, hoping the activity will distract him from his hunger - but fish and chips is the only thing on his mind. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Robert Fyfe and Juliette Kaplan

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In the final episode of Roy Clarke's long-running comedy, everyone prepares for the wedding - prompting Howard and Pearl to look back at their relationship. Meanwhile, Alvin flirts with Stella, Toby tries to smarten himself up in a bid to win back Monica, and Clegg worries he has forgotten something important

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A concerned mother hires Hetty to find out who is stalking her daughter just days before her wedding. It's not long before the unlikely sleuth is on the trail of a disgraced soldier with a grudge against the father of the bride-to-be. Light-hearted drama, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam learns Gene has planted evidence on a notorious armed robber, and decides to let him go. However, the crook then continues his crime spree, critically wounding a cleaner from the police station in the process. The idealistic detective begins to question whether his colleague's approach might be justified - but fears his doubts could affect his chances of returning to the 21st century

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: A man admitted to hospital after a traffic accident turns out to be one of two children who disappeared without trace 15 years previously. At the time, a nationwide manhunt failed to produce any answers, and now the cold case squad investigates where the missing boy has been all this time and whether his twin sister is still alive. Detective drama, starring Trevor Eve

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The Sun Hill cops spring into action when an elderly Alzheimer's sufferer goes missing along with her five-month-old great-granddaughter. programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

programme.title: Caru Siopa

Carwen and Dylan love a bargain, but here they are challenged to buy clothes from charity shops before transforming them and selling them on and the winner keeps all the cash. Lara passes on clever tips to encourage everyone to recycle favourite fashion items

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: Catching up with the Cleary family seven years after they appeared on the show to see if they decided to leave the UK behind for a new life in Australia

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: CCTV footage from a petrol station helps convict a gang of cold-blooded killers. Elsewhere, a criminal's mobile phone finally uncovers a gang targeting the vulnerable

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: John Cameron and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Detling, Kent, hoping to profit at an auction in nearby Cranbrook. Plus, presenter Charlie Ross offers viewers a few top tips when making their first auction purchase

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown must race to save Bunty from the hangman's noose when she is tried for murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams and Emer Kenny

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in the Shropshire countryside with a sociable couple who have £425,000 to invest in a home that gives them space for entertaining and their beloved jukebox

programme.title: President Biden: The Inauguration
programme.desc: A live report from Washington, DC, as Joe Biden is sworn in as the 46th President of the United States, and Kamala Harris takes the oath of office as the new Vice President

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items. This time, seller Pam arrives with a vintage pommel horse, typical of ones that used to be a feature of all school gyms, before Lorraine presents a mid-20th century French wedding globe. Later, Gary hopes to impress the dealers with his vintage table hockey game

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Dominic Chinea undertakes the challenging restoration of a Carrom board, a traditional Asian board game, while Steven Kember teams up with silversmith Brenton West to repair a precious silver menorah. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher restores a small wooden duck once used in magic tricks, and bookbinder Christopher Shaw repairs a pair of photo albums charting Captain Scott's Antarctic expedition

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Fulham v Manchester United at Craven Cottage and Manchester City v Aston Villa at Etihad Stadium, as both Manchester clubs looked for victories to aid their title challenges. Plus, Leeds United v Southampton, West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion and Leicester City v Chelsea

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel Esports enter their second week of the season, and the players start to miss home. YoungBuck feels there is a stigma in the gaming community for those who have a girlfriend

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford investigate lockdown issues, including why some people have had trouble getting refunds for cancelled concerts

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martell Maxwell follow the progress of renovation projects in Stonebroom in Derbyshire, Pontypridd in Wales and Cobridge in Staffordshire

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Lee and Abigail Parsons, who hoped a new life in New Zealand would mean an escape from their hectic lives in the UK

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a simple business card revealed the true identities of a gang of con artists, and a ripped-up note that held the key to locking away two murderers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Edinburgh, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visiting the city's famous underground vaults

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Murder proves to be no laughing matter when Father Brown visits a jesters' convention. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams as the sleuthing priest, with Neil Pearson and Paul Copley

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prove that home-made can beat shop-bought when they prepare gravlax, jam doughnuts and their own corned beef

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a Suffolk house hunter find a low-maintenance rural home with a wildlife-friendly garden for £490,000. Plus, a look at traditional boatbuilding skills

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: At one of the UK's oldest and biggest livestock markets, in the town of Melton Mowbray, sheep farmer Elizabeth - with her partner Mitchel - will be hoping to impress her father by selling four of their breeding rams. Meanwhile, experienced mum and daughter Sheila and Julie intend to cement their national reputation for sheep breeding by selling theirs. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on a plate with a famous name on it, a light from an operating theatre, a puzzle box and some Chinese silver - as well as a quirky orange item

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Determined to go through with her plan, Sharon tells Ian she is making a special dinner. He seems wary - until she tells him they should finally consummate their marriage. Jay apologises to Honey for the hurtful things he said to Billy about their kiss. Iqra is disgusted by Ash's betrayal and ends their relationship, and with nowhere else to go, Ash is forced to go home to her family

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as four more professional dog groomers are put to the test. First up in the Breed Makeover round are four English cocker spaniels, as the contestants try to tame the gun dogs' wig and furnishings. They are then challenged to create a tricky spiral around the coats of four frizzy-haired models, before parading them on the dogwalk, where their owners see the results for the first time. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It seems money doesn't buy happiness when lottery winner Cherry is found dead at her luxurious villa, much to the horror of her husband and their visiting friends. As usual, Neville and the team are presented with a real head-scratcher when it turns out no one was in the house at the time - and the mystery deepens when the victim's body apparently vanishes into thin air. Meanwhile, JP is training unruly new officer Marlon, aware that his wife Rosey is due to give birth at any time. Kelvin Fletcher, Jason Manford and Faye McKeever guest star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. He also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville drill down into more of the topics affecting viewers in lockdown, investigating the struggles experienced with some of the biggest names in home delivery. Plus, with pandemic restrictions leading to a boom in cycling, they take a look at the problems encountered by some people trying to get hold of a bike

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of properties sold at auction in Gorleston in Norfolk, Dover in Kent and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands. All of the developers have high hopes to make money from their property inverstmants

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Lee and Debbie Daniels had been through their fair share of problems and thought a new life in New Zealand might be the answer. Five years on, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they decided to make the move

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded gun cartridge assisted police in convicting a man who had gunned down a fellow officer. Plus, the packet of medication that helped lock up a gang of violent jewellery thieves. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion observes which side raises the greater profit and also looks at the history of English jigsaw company Victory Games

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After a mysterious thief keeps stealing valuable treasures from under his nose, Hercule Flambeau visits Kembleford to see if Father Brown can help him to eliminate the competition. Mark Williams and John Light star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate food from Northumberland, including local seafood, a traditional lamb recipe and a new way of serving Si's favourite ice-cream

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in Shropshire with a garlic specialist and his Russian wife who want to establish a new business that taps into the county's thriving food scene, though not before securing their dream family home in the country on a £550,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali gets in tune with the region's melodic heritage when she learns about the expert craft of harp-making

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Young farmer Izzi took over from her father aged just 22. She's responsible for a herd of 43 Highland cows and some rare Lincoln Reds. Sisters Laura and Samantha started making award-winning wines on 12 acres of their father's farm four years ago. With lots of overheads and plans for their farms' futures, these women head to the beautiful Creake Abbey Famers' Market to try and fill the coffers. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on two very decorative perfume bottles, an unusual box, two show-stopping pieces of African art, some rare ceramics and a unique light fitting

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich compete to design a £6,000 Italian-themed garden for a couple in Fareham, Hampshire. The designers' creative skills are going to be tested to the limit if the uninspiring grass patch is to become a Mediterranean idyll

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It's judgement day for Ian as Sharon finally puts the finishing touches to her revenge plan and indulges her husband in an especially deadly dinner. Mick finds himself in an impossible situation

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Josh Widdicombe, presenter Gemma Cairney, auctioneer Raj Bisram and fashion designer Sophie Hermann join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Frank announces his engagement, but Lee cannot believe that any woman would want to marry his father so Lucy invites Frank and his beau Carol to dinner to allay Lee's misgivings. When Carol turns out to be perfect, Lee becomes even more nervous fearing that if she dumps his dad, it'll be his shoulder that he will cry on. In an effort to determine Carol's motivations, Lee joins Frank's dating app and puts himself forward as a potential date for her. If she bites, he will know she's up to no good. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Bobby Ball and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance by Susie Blake

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host invites award-winning actress Carey Mulligan to talk about her role in the revenge thriller Promising Young Woman. Hollywood and Broadway star Neil Patrick Harris also guests, discussing his appearance in Russell T Davies' 1980s-set serial It's a Sin. Plus, ex-footballer Ian Wright promotes his podcast is Ian Wright's Everyday People, Grantchester star James Norton reflects on his role in the powerful drama Nowhere Special and French actress Camille Cottin also guests. Singer-songwriter Celeste performs her current single Love Is Back

programme.title: Interview with the Vampire (1994)
programme.desc: A man who claims to be 200 years old tells a reporter his story of how he was bitten during an encounter with a decadent and charismatic vampire in 18th-century New Orleans. He relates how his respect for the sanctity of human life clashed with his blood-lust, and how the pair came to be adoptive parents to a young girl. Gothic horror based on the novel by Anne Rice, with Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, Kirsten Dunst, Antonio Banderas and Christian Slater

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Property series catching up with UK families after their week in Australia or New Zealand to discover whether they decided to make the move and live there permanently

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Donna Mullings and Ahmed Mudawi meet members of the deaf community to learn about their experiences of racism and how it can be dismantled. With voiceover

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a layered chocolate and peanut butter cake, and uses liquorice pellets to make a sauce for a Basque burnt cheesecake. She also demonstrates a recipe for roast vegetables with cumin and fennel seeds to accompany spiced bulgur wheat and beetroot sauce

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: With Katie Thistleton

programme.title: Magic Numbers: Hannah Fry's Mysterious World of Maths
programme.desc: As her journey examining whether maths was invented or discovered concludes, Mathematician Dr Hannah Fry explores a paradox at the heart of modern maths, and what maths can reveal about the fundamental building blocks of the universe

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring a ladies' singles semi-final and a second-round open singles match. Eventual ladies' champion Julie Forrest defeated Alison Merrien at this stage last year

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns get a taste of running a store in the 1920s, 30s and 40s, considered the golden age of the corner shop due to the plentiful availability of packaged food. However, rationing quickly has a devastating impact on both the shop and the family's day-to-day lives, with carrot lollies and mutton bacon proving less than enticing meals. The Arderns learn how to make ice cream by hand and blend tea, as well as taking delivery of a vintage fruit machine. They also find themselves at the centre of a community party recreating the VE Day celebrations

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham catches up on the action from the live cameras in the New Forest and on the River Ness in Scotland, while Iolo Williams follows a feral flock of barnacle geese. Elsewhere, cameras delve below the surface of Loch Carron to see the amazing brittlestars who thrive there in winter

programme.title: Targeted: The Truth about Disability Hate Crime
programme.desc: Disabled filmmaker Richard Butchins' brutally honest film explores the abuse and aggression faced by disabled people in everyday life, from verbal insults to violent physical attacks. The film features a wide range of testimony from survivors themselves as they share their own experiences of the abuse they have faced

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A nostalgic look back at the early part of the decade as the music show embraced the advent of 'new pop', by changing its theme tune and titles and parting company with dance act Legs & Co. Acts featured include Duran Duran, Depeche Mode, the Human League, Kim Wilde, Midge Ure, Jerry Dammers (of the Specials), Beggar and Co, Leee John and Freeez. With Richard Skinner, Mike Read and Carrie Grant

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. With Emily Maitlis and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Silenced: The Hidden Story of Disabled Britain
programme.desc: Cerrie Burnell presents a history of disabled people's struggle for human rights in Britain. The film looks back to the policies in place in Victorian workhouses, uncovering the original records of an institution created to segregate disabled people and stop them from having children. She also shares inspiring stories of pioneering campaigners for social change, and looks at the challenges still to be faced in the future

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, including the ladies' singles semi-finals and the second round of the open singles

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs ends in Finland, where she discovers that climate change has had a serious impact on many traditional trades. In the south of the country, she explores the timber trade by meeting a forest cleaner and a baker. Venturing north, where temperatures are rising the most, she gets to grips with the tourist industry by training as an ice carver and an elf in Santa's village

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Donna Mullings and Ahmed Mudawi meet members of the deaf community to learn about their experiences of racism and how it can be dismantled. With voiceover

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A scribbled name in a discarded notebook is the key to putting a dangerous driver behind bars, while the serial numbers on stolen cash cracks the mystery of a dangerous gang

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with the Cleary family seven years after they appeared on the show to see if they decided to leave the UK behind for a new life in Australia

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles look at Chinese ceramics with an eye-watering price tag

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Dog fanatic Sheridan Smith and her fluffy co-host Stanley are on a mission to find the nation's best professional dog groomer. Heat one sees the first four contestants working on a long-haired shih-tzu - one of the most popular breeds to grace British salons. After that they let their imaginations fly as they give four curly haired canines a teddy bear trim to parade on the dogwalk in front of their owners

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Cel Spellman presents Thinking Thursday, featuring help with cyberbullying

programme.title: The Joy of Data with Dr Hannah Fry
programme.desc: The mathematician explores the world of data, revealing exactly what it is and how it is captured, stored, shared and made sense of. She tells the story of the engineers of the data age, who brought about a technological and philosophical revolution, and confesses that, for her, the joy of data is all about spotting patterns

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the ladies' singles final and a second-round open singles match. Julie Forrest retained the ladies' title last year with a victory over Janice Gower

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Burton Constable Hall in the East Riding of Yorkshire, where antiques expert Jonathan Pratt finds a bronze statue and Catherine Southon enthuses about a silver bowl. Paul also finds out about the life of poet Philip Larkin

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penrith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwall's oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The classicist returns with the cultural review show, and hot on the heels of Joe Biden's inauguration, she talks to Veep and The Thick of It creator Armando Iannucci to ask how comedians and satirists adjust to a new regime. She is also joined by David Olusoga, who recently interviewed Barack Obama, to explore succession through the ages

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson celebrates the food she loves to cook for friends and family at home, demonstrating a wide range of recipes that are warming, comforting and inspirational. She shares new discoveries like her Turkish-inspired eggs, old-fashioned favourites including the aptly named Queen of Puddings, as well as her emergency brownies. Nigella also rustles up a simple chicken and pea tray-bake for a relaxed get-together around the table, and she finds a new use for a spiraliser

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Reports on the wonderful wildlife and seasonal beauty to be found in winter. Chris Packham is in the New Forest looking at the shape of nests and different species of bee, Iolo Williams looks at tree creepers and Gillian Burke reports from the Beaver Project in Cornwall. Cameraman Richard Taylor-Jones tells the story of the Oare Marshes on the River Swale, which has gone from a site making gunpowder to a wildlife haven

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks a completely random selection of questions this week, with Bill Bailey, Daliso Chaponda, Sally Phillips and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as Angela Barnes, Catherine Bohart, Ed Gamble, Rhys James and Michael Odewale join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look back on the latest events in news and politics. Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Gina Yashere comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. She warms up the audience before introducing routines by 2018 Britain's Got Talent winner Lost Voice Guy and Mock the Week regular Ed Gamble

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall

programme.title: Targeted: The Truth about Disability Hate Crime
programme.desc: Disabled filmmaker Richard Butchins' brutally honest film explores the abuse and aggression faced by disabled people in everyday life, from verbal insults to violent physical attacks. The film features a wide range of testimony from survivors themselves as they share their own experiences of the abuse they have faced

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, including the ladies' singles final

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Solent, one the busiest stretches of water in the UK, reports come in of a missing kite surfer. The crew of Lymington lifeboat station start searching, but as the hours pass they begin to fear the worst. Twenty miles to the west, the Poole crew are called out to save a father and daughter from drowning as they are swept out to sea, and at Chiswick, one of the few RNLI stations with a crew on permanent standby 24/7, an unusual casualty in the Thames leaves the crew attempting to save not just one but nine lives

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The documentary charting the transformation of celebrity examines how people capitalised on the digital revolution in order to sidestep the traditional routes to fame. When the old tabloid machinery began to lose power, celebrities made the move to the digital press - and soon after pop acts were making a buzz on the internet. Meanwhile, structured reality shows dominated the younger demographic - and all of these things came together through social media. All of a sudden there was a new way of becoming famous

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Lee and Abigail Parsons, who hoped a new life in New Zealand would mean an escape from their hectic lives in the UK

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: At one of the UK's oldest and biggest livestock markets, in the town of Melton Mowbray, sheep farmer Elizabeth - with her partner Mitchel - will be hoping to impress her father by selling four of their breeding rams. Meanwhile, experienced mum and daughter Sheila and Julie intend to cement their national reputation for sheep breeding by selling theirs. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park codebreaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered Victorian firefighter's helmet. Woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to repair a 19th-century stereoscope viewer that sparked a lifelong obsession, and soft toy rest toy restorers Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell try to make a faceless old teddy bear smile again

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international update

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Teachers provide curriculum support for 11-14-year-olds. Presented by Cel Spellman

programme.title: Calculating Ada: The Countess of Computing
programme.desc: Dr Hannah Fry tells the story of Ada Lovelace, the daughter of Lord Byron and a scandalous socialite. But when Charles Babbage designed the world's first steam-powered computers, most famously the analytical engine, it was Ada who realised the full potential of these machines

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the second and third open singles quarter-finals. Les Gillett and Nick Brett were among the victors at this stage last year, overcoming David Gourlay and Mark Royal respectively

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Barrow Hill Roundhouse near Chesterfield in Derbyshire. Antiques experts Jonathan Pratt and Raj Bisram are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Jonathan comes across a collection of 1950s powder compacts, while Raj discovers some railway signal lamps. Paul finds out about the history of the Roundhouse and gets hands on with some locomotive maintenance

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: As the chef's Cornish odyssey nears its end, he takes a break in the city of Truro, where he discovers an unusual ghost story and tours the magnificent cathedral. He also meets a family passionate about offal, who prepare Rick a meal of pickled ox tongue and beef heart tacos, and in the county's most northerly parish he tells the story of an eccentric vicar who would retrieve the bodies from nearby shipwrecks and bury them in his church graveyard

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Billy Bragg pays the Arderns a visit as they get to grips with 1960s life. The new decade brings a dizzying number of new developments, including skiffle music, hula hoops and sliced bread. It also brought about a seismic change in shopping with the introduction of self-service. The rise of supermarkets sees the personal service of the Arderns' shop under threat, forcing them to diversify their stock. The 1960s saw Sheffield becoming home to new workers from across the Commonwealth, many of whom share their memories of the era and the importance of the local corner shop to shift workers

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Megan McCubbin joins Chris Packham in the New Forest, catching up on the drama showing up on the live cameras at his badger sett and the spawning salmon in the River Ness. Chris also takes a look at the reasons why waders bob. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some amazing sights and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver Project in Cornwall

programme.title: The Investigation
programme.desc: Copenhagen chief of homicide Jens Moller learns of a missing Swedish journalist who has not returned from interviewing the designer of a home-made submarine. Initial garbled reports indicate that she and the designer have returned to harbour, but this proves to be false, as only the designer is rescued before the vessel sinks. A search for the woman is launched, but Moller decides to charge his suspect with murder, despite the lack of a body. Months of intricate detective work follow to ensure that justice is obtained. Soren Malling stars in this drama based on a true story. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: The Investigation
programme.desc: The police incident room gets flooded with calls, as Jens Moller and his team follow up various leads regarding the case. Forensics experts have discovered traces of human blood in the raised sub, which has to be analysed. Maibritt calls the various sailing clubs to attempt to identify a yacht seen close to the submarine, and Nikolaj tries to trace the route of the submarine on its final and seemingly fatal voyage. Meanwhile, Jens contacts the missing journalist's parents to ask for access to their daughter's computers. Soren Malling, Laura Christensen and Hans Henrik Clemensen star. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton is on Barra, where he witnesses some of the great spectacles of the natural world

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. Presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The inhabitants of New Caprica cling to the hope that Adama and his fleet will rescue them following the occupation of the settlement by Cylons - and the committed Admiral plans a risky assault to save those he left behind. Sci-fi drama, starring Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Tigh's extreme tactics leave the human survivors appalled and lead to a brutal crackdown by the Cylon leaders. Adama tries to launch a risky mission to meet up with the resistance, but encounters opposition from Lee. Meanwhile, Leoben brings a visitor to see Kara. Sci-fi drama, starring Edward James Olmos

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles quarter-finals

programme.title: Timbuktu (2014)
programme.desc: A cattle herder living a peaceful life in the desert with his wife and children accidentally crosses paths with the Jihadists who control the nearby ancient town of Timbuktu. Overnight, his world is shattered, and everything he loves and values is threatened. Drama, starring Ibrahim Ahmed, Abel Jafri and Toulou Kiki. In French, Arabic, Bambara, English and Songhay

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Mark Daly investigates why black men in the UK are more likely than white men to have force used on them by police, and to die in police custody. He follows the family of Kevin Clarke on their search for justice after Mr Clarke repeatedly said 'I can't breathe' as he was restrained by police on the ground for 14 minutes during a mental health crisis, and died soon afterwards. The programme also reveals fresh evidence in Scotland's most high-profile death in custody after Sheku Bayoh died in 2015 after being restrained by up to six officers

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and mathematician Hannah Fry take over a restaurant and invite five celebrities to enjoy a dinner party with a difference, where they will be scored on the carbon footprint of every dish they choose. Diners Sara Pascoe, Amol Rajan, Nikki Fox, Desiree Burch and Matthew Fort choose from a menu of tantalising treats, each of which tells its own environmental story

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack fulfils a personal ambition by driving his traction engine across the Scammonden Bridge near Huddersfield, West Yorkshire. He also visits a forge in Sheffield and watches crucible steel being made by the experts at Abbeydale Industrial Hamlet

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a warm, elegant snow-kissed scene, with a little cabin snuggled deeply in the embrace of foothills, bushes and evergreen trees

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: The second episode focuses on the monarch's relationship with her daughters after the death of Prince Albert, exploring how Victoria clung to and bullied them, as well as arranging who they should marry. The princesses' response was to fight back, making them unlikely champions of female independence

programme.title: Victoria & Abdul (2017)
programme.desc: Adbul Karim is whisked from his clerk's job to present a ceremonial coin to Queen Victoria for her golden jubilee. The monarch, who is tired of her fawning courtiers, takes a liking to the young man and promotes him to become her Munshi, or spiritual teacher - much to the resentment of her family and household. Period drama, starring Judi Dench, Ali Fazal, Tim Pigott-Smith and Eddie Izzard

programme.title: Judi Dench: All the World's Her Stage
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the life and times of the veteran actress, looking back at the high points of her 60-year career. Billy Connolly recalls the time, when filming Mrs Brown, he thought she was falling in love with him, while Samantha Bond remembers how she calmed Pierce Brosnan's nerves to prepare him for his first scene with the actress. Other contributors include Daniel Craig, Ann Mitchell, Sam Mendes and Ian McKellen. Narrated by Colin Salmon

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Chris Lintott look back at some of the biggest stories of 2020, from the launch of Solar Orbiter to the discovery of phosphine on Venus

programme.title: Night on Film - An A-Z of the Dark
programme.desc: An alphabetical compilation of footage about nocturnal life, featuring everything from bats to vampires as the night comes alive in this unusual mixture of music and archive

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a warm, elegant snow-kissed scene, with a little cabin snuggled deeply in the embrace of foothills, bushes and evergreen trees

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack fulfils a personal ambition by driving his traction engine across the Scammonden Bridge near Huddersfield, West Yorkshire. He also visits a forge in Sheffield and watches crucible steel being made by the experts at Abbeydale Industrial Hamlet

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: The second episode focuses on the monarch's relationship with her daughters after the death of Prince Albert, exploring how Victoria clung to and bullied them, as well as arranging who they should marry. The princesses' response was to fight back, making them unlikely champions of female independence

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack investigates traditional craftsmanship in Yorkshire and Derbyshire, including glass-cutting, making rivets using the traditional hot forge method, and ornate carving. He also catches up with some old friends in the process of restoring a showman's engine and watches workers at the Midland Railway Centre as they repair damaged trains

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a snow-covered forest, highlighted by winter's rustic touches of colour to add a bit of warmth and beauty

programme.title: The Bermuda Triangle: Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: Bruce Denardo explores the legend of the region where many planes and ships have disappeared in mysterious circumstances. Using a state-of-the-art submarine, Graham Hawkes reveals how five wrecks ended up 730ft down in the heart of the deadly expanse of water

programme.title: All the President's Men (1976)
programme.desc: The true story of Washington Post reporters Bob Woodward and Carl Bernstein, who persevered in their investigation of the links between the Watergate break-in and the White House, a scandal that eventually forced the resignation of President Nixon. Alan J Pakula's drama, starring Robert Redford, Dustin Hoffman, Jason Robards and Hal Holbrook

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions within a genre that mixes film and music and encompasses many forms, from drama and comedy to fantasy and documentary. There is the classic pop star vehicle, where the biggest acts play themselves, or a version of themselves, as well as dramatised accounts of the stars' lives that dazzle with musical set pieces, while playing on the fascination with the darker side of fame

programme.title: 'Til Kingdom Come: Trump, Faith and Money
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the close ties between US evangelicals and Israel's foremost philanthropic organisation, and its influence on both nations' foreign policies

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a snow-covered forest, highlighted by winter's rustic touches of colour to add a bit of warmth and beauty

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack investigates traditional craftsmanship in Yorkshire and Derbyshire, including glass-cutting, making rivets using the traditional hot forge method, and ornate carving. He also catches up with some old friends in the process of restoring a showman's engine and watches workers at the Midland Railway Centre as they repair damaged trains

programme.title: The Bermuda Triangle: Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: Bruce Denardo explores the legend of the region where many planes and ships have disappeared in mysterious circumstances. Using a state-of-the-art submarine, Graham Hawkes reveals how five wrecks ended up 730ft down in the heart of the deadly expanse of water

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and author reveals how the modern shape of the musical was established through a series of pioneering works, examining Jerome Kern and Oscar Hammerstein's Show Boat's bold look at America's racial divide in the 1920s. He also explores innovative uses of songs that further the narrative, referencing Alan Jay Lerner and Frederick Loewe's My Fair Lady, which made a star of Julie Andrews in the late 1950s

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on July 5, with performances by Inspiral Carpets, Janet Jackson, Poison, Double Trouble, MC Tunes versus 808 State, FAB featuring MC Parker, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, Massivo featuring Tracy, MC Hammer, Elton John and Glenn Medeiros featuring Bobby Brown

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on July 12, with performances by Gun, River City People, Blue Pearl, Glenn Medeiros featuring Bobby Brown, Thunder, the Soup Dragons featuring Junior Reid, the Stone Roses, Elton John and Technotronic featuring Ya Kid K

programme.title: Neil Diamond at the BBC
programme.desc: The pico of the iconic American singer-songwriter's performances for the broadcaster over the decades. This compilation features performances from the early 1970s of hits like Sweet Caroline, Forever in Blue Jeans and Solitary Man, and appearances on a range of BBC programmes including Wogan, The Shirley Bassey Show, Later with Jools Holland and Top of the Pops. Alongside all the hits are some rarely seen interview clips that capture Neil's feelings about his enduring career and show how his dislike of fame has always sat in conflict with his passion for performing the songs that he and his fans love so much

programme.title: Neil Diamond at Glastonbury 2008
programme.desc: A performance from the iconic festival, focusing on the American singer-songwriter's set on the Pyramid Stage at the event held in June at Worthy Farm in Somerset. Songs performed include I'm a Believer, Sweet Caroline and Red Red Wine, plus a couple of numbers from his album Home Before Dark

programme.title: Neil Diamond: Electric Prom
programme.desc: The singer-songwriter performs a selection of his greatest hits and cover versions from his album Dreams, accompanied by a six-piece band, at London's Roundhouse in 2010. The concert explores the songs he loves from the 1960s and '70s, and reinvents his classic tracks

programme.title: Sings Neil Diamond
programme.desc: An archive collection of performances from artists covering tracks by Neil Diamond. Including Red Red Wine by UB40, Urge Overkill's version of Girl, You'll Be a Woman Soon, Robert Wyatt's take on I'm a Believer and Lulu singing Boat That I Row

programme.title: The Joy of Easy Listening
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of the genre, from its emergence in the 1950s to its heyday in the 60s, through its survival in the subsequent two decades and revival in the 90s. The programme explores the people behind the songs and the mark they have left on modern life. Featuring interviews with Engelbert Humperdinck, Richard Carpenter and Jimmy Webb

programme.title: Neil Diamond at the BBC
programme.desc: The pico of the iconic American singer-songwriter's performances for the broadcaster over the decades. programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she discovers that astronauts live in space on a spacecraft called the International Space Station and that there have been different astronauts living there for over 20 years. Maddie shows us how astronauts live and work on the International Space Station and do lots of different jobs and research in order to learn more about space and our planet Earth. We explore a laboratory where they do experiments, the food they eat, how they wash and even how they go to the toilet. We find out that the space station is made from thousands of parts that were put together by astronauts in space, and we learn that it’s also a home because it’s a place where astronauts eat, sleep and have fun.

programme.title: CBeebies Stargazing
programme.desc: Inspired by the Voyager missions, CBeebies Stargazing: Space Explorers takes pre-schoolers on a journey of discovery through the Solar System. Robert the Robot instructs Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers to set a course for the next planet on their journey, Saturn. Before setting off, Maggie explains how excited people were when Voyager sent back the first pictures of Saturn and its rings. In Mission Control, Robert receives another incoming message from Astronomer Edward who uploads some mission data of Saturn and asks Stargazers everywhere to try and spot its rings. Back on Space Sailor, Chris and Maggie are still on their way to Saturn. Maggie explains that it took Voyager over three years to get there but points out that there are lots of sights to enjoy while they make their journey, like the sunlight lighting up the canal. More Stargazers send an update back to Mission Control to inform Robert that the Sun’s light also lights up Saturn’s icy rings. Finally reaching their destinati

programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal. While Ubercorn explains how the crater was formed by a meteorite, Foz searches it for fragments of the space rock, as objects from space often contain precous metals. But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out how the robots astronauts use in space have helped us to create robots we use here on Earth. We discover there are lots of different types of space robots, robonauts that help astronauts to do jobs, space probes that travel to far off parts of the solar system, and landers and rovers - robots that land on planets like Mars to collect information. We learn how robots are sent on special missions to Mars and how they take pictures and send information back to Earth. Maddie visits a research centre to meet a Mars rover robot and uses her special cameras to help us see how it moves over the ground.

programme.title: CBeebies Stargazing
programme.desc: Inspired by the Voyager missions, CBeebies Stargazing: Space Explorers takes pre-schoolers on a journey of discovery through the Solar System. Robert the Robot tells Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers that the next stop on their journey is Uranus. Maggie explains that it took Voyager 2 five years to make the journey from Saturn to Uranus – it’s nearly three billion kilometres away from earth! In Mission Control, Robert receives another incoming message from Astronomer Edward, who uploads some pictures of Uranus from his telescope for Stargazers everywhere to look at. Back on the Space Sailor, Chris is amazed at how far Uranus is from Earth. Maggie explains that it could’ve been a lot further if it weren’t for the planets’ alignments being just right for Voyager 2’s mission. More Stargazers send an update back to Mission Control to inform Robert that like Saturn, Uranus also has rings! Keen to see the rings up close, Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers arrive at Uranus and discover another S

Maddie explains the different layers of our atmosphere and how astronauts travel through it into space and the special suits they wear to protect them. She tries on a real spacesuit and learns about the types of spacesuits they wear to do different jobs. Maddie discovers astronauts need to be fit, healthy and have good agility. She finds out how they learn how to move in space when they can’t keep their feet on the ground! We discover there is less gravity in space than on Earth and although astronauts look like they are floating, they are free falling.

programme.title: CBeebies Stargazing
programme.desc: Inspired by the Voyager missions, CBeebies Stargazing: Space Explorers takes pre-schoolers on a journey of discovery through the Solar System. Robert the Robot tells Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers that the final stop on their journey is the planet Neptune. Maggie explains Neptune is the last planet in our solar system, over four billion kilometres away from planet Earth. In Mission Control, Robert receives a final update from Astronomer Edward, who uploads a picture of Neptune for Stargazers everywhere to look at. Just as Robert is about to enjoy a tasty cake, another message comes into Mission Control. This time it's from Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers, they’ve made it to Neptune! Maggie then reveals that when Voyager made it to Neptune, it discovered that there were extreme winds on the planet. programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. He also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville drill down into more of the topics affecting viewers in lockdown, investigating the struggles experienced with some of the biggest names in home delivery. Plus, with pandemic restrictions leading to a boom in cycling, they take a look at the problems encountered by some people trying to get hold of a bike

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of properties sold at auction in Gorleston in Norfolk, Dover in Kent and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands. All of the developers have high hopes to make money from their property inverstmants

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded gun cartridge assisted police in convicting a man who had gunned down a fellow officer. Plus, the packet of medication that helped lock up a gang of violent jewellery thieves. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion observes which side raises the greater profit and also looks at the history of English jigsaw company Victory Games

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After a mysterious thief keeps stealing valuable treasures from under his nose, Hercule Flambeau visits Kembleford to see if Father Brown can help him to eliminate the competition. Mark Williams and John Light star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate food from Northumberland, including local seafood, a traditional lamb recipe and a new way of serving Si's favourite ice-cream

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Lee and Debbie Daniels had been through their fair share of problems and thought a new life in New Zealand might be the answer. Five years on, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they decided to make the move

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Young farmer Izzi took over from her father aged just 22. She's responsible for a herd of 43 Highland cows and some rare Lincoln Reds. Sisters Laura and Samantha started making award-winning wines on 12 acres of their father's farm four years ago. With lots of overheads and plans for their farms' futures, these women head to the beautiful Creake Abbey Famers' Market to try and fill the coffers. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on two very decorative perfume bottles, an unusual box, two show-stopping pieces of African art, some rare ceramics and a unique light fitting

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard takes to the dance floor to make a vegan meal that will get a troupe of ballroom dancers foxtrotting to a straight 10. With a sprinkle of flare, a dollop of panache and a pinch of style, Matt aims to show the waltzing wonders how plant-based food can stand up to any conventional British classic dish

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It's judgement day for Ian as Sharon finally puts the finishing touches to her revenge plan and indulges her husband in an especially deadly dinner. Mick finds himself in an impossible situation

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Josh Widdicombe, presenter Gemma Cairney, auctioneer Raj Bisram and fashion designer Sophie Hermann join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Frank announces his engagement, but Lee cannot believe that any woman would want to marry his father so Lucy invites Frank and his beau Carol to dinner to allay Lee's misgivings. When Carol turns out to be perfect, Lee becomes even more nervous fearing that if she dumps his dad, it'll be his shoulder that he will cry on. In an effort to determine Carol's motivations, Lee joins Frank's dating app and puts himself forward as a potential date for her. If she bites, he will know she's up to no good. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Bobby Ball and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance by Susie Blake

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host invites award-winning actress Carey Mulligan to talk about her role in the revenge thriller Promising Young Woman. Hollywood and Broadway star Neil Patrick Harris also guests, discussing his appearance in Russell T Davies' 1980s-set serial It's a Sin. Plus, ex-footballer Ian Wright promotes his podcast is Ian Wright's Everyday People, Grantchester star James Norton reflects on his role in the powerful drama Nowhere Special and French actress Camille Cottin also guests. Singer-songwriter Celeste performs her current single Love Is Back

programme.title: Interview with the Vampire (1994)
programme.desc: A man who claims to be 200 years old tells a reporter his story of how he was bitten during an encounter with a decadent and charismatic vampire in 18th-century New Orleans. He relates how his respect for the sanctity of human life clashed with his blood-lust, and how the pair came to be adoptive parents to a young girl. Gothic horror based on the novel by Anne Rice, with Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, Kirsten Dunst, Antonio Banderas and Christian Slater

programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher restores a small wooden duck once used in magic tricks, and bookbinder Christopher Shaw repairs a pair of photo albums charting Captain Scott's Antarctic expedition

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Vigilant beat cops Fletcher and McLaren recover the same drugs twice. Comedy, starring Jack Docherty, Grado and Karen Bartke

programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as four more professional dog groomers are put to the test. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It seems money doesn't buy happiness when lottery winner Cherry is found dead at her luxurious villa, much to the horror of her husband and their visiting friends.
