programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. He also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville drill down into more of the topics affecting viewers in lockdown, investigating the struggles experienced with some of the biggest names in home delivery. Plus, with pandemic restrictions leading to a boom in cycling, they take a look at the problems encountered by some people trying to get hold of a bike

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of properties sold at auction in Gorleston in Norfolk, Dover in Kent and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands. All of the developers have high hopes to make money from their property inverstmants

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Lee and Debbie Daniels had been through their fair share of problems and thought a new life in New Zealand might be the answer. Five years on, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they decided to make the move

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded gun cartridge assisted police in convicting a man who had gunned down a fellow officer. Plus, the packet of medication that helped lock up a gang of violent jewellery thieves. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion observes which side raises the greater profit and also looks at the history of English jigsaw company Victory Games

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After a mysterious thief keeps stealing valuable treasures from under his nose, Hercule Flambeau visits Kembleford to see if Father Brown can help him to eliminate the competition. Mark Williams and John Light star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate food from Northumberland, including local seafood, a traditional lamb recipe and a new way of serving Si's favourite ice-cream

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in Shropshire with a garlic specialist and his Russian wife who want to establish a new business that taps into the county's thriving food scene, though not before securing their dream family home in the country on a £550,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali gets in tune with the region's melodic heritage when she learns about the expert craft of harp-making

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Young farmer Izzi took over from her father aged just 22. She's responsible for a herd of 43 Highland cows and some rare Lincoln Reds. Sisters Laura and Samantha started making award-winning wines on 12 acres of their father's farm four years ago. With lots of overheads and plans for their farms' futures, these women head to the beautiful Creake Abbey Famers' Market to try and fill the coffers. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on two very decorative perfume bottles, an unusual box, two show-stopping pieces of African art, some rare ceramics and a unique light fitting

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich compete to design a £6,000 Italian-themed garden for a couple in Fareham, Hampshire. The designers' creative skills are going to be tested to the limit if the uninspiring grass patch is to become a Mediterranean idyll

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It's judgement day for Ian as Sharon finally puts the finishing touches to her revenge plan and indulges her husband in an especially deadly dinner. Mick finds himself in an impossible situation

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Josh Widdicombe, presenter Gemma Cairney, auctioneer Raj Bisram and fashion designer Sophie Hermann join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Frank announces his engagement, but Lee cannot believe that any woman would want to marry his father so Lucy invites Frank and his beau Carol to dinner to allay Lee's misgivings. When Carol turns out to be perfect, Lee becomes even more nervous fearing that if she dumps his dad, it'll be his shoulder that he will cry on. In an effort to determine Carol's motivations, Lee joins Frank's dating app and puts himself forward as a potential date for her. If she bites, he will know she's up to no good. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Bobby Ball and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance by Susie Blake

programme.title: Give My Head Peace
programme.desc: Pastor Begbie sees double when his twin brother Pavel turns up - who's a Polish priest. Award-winning comedy, starring Tim McGarry and Paddy Jenkins

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host invites award-winning actress Carey Mulligan to talk about her role in the revenge thriller Promising Young Woman. Hollywood and Broadway star Neil Patrick Harris also guests, discussing his appearance in Russell T Davies' 1980s-set serial It's a Sin. Plus, ex-footballer Ian Wright promotes his podcast is Ian Wright's Everyday People, Grantchester star James Norton reflects on his role in the powerful drama Nowhere Special and French actress Camille Cottin also guests. Singer-songwriter Celeste performs her current single Love Is Back

programme.title: Interview with the Vampire (1994)
programme.desc: A man who claims to be 200 years old tells a reporter his story of how he was bitten during an encounter with a decadent and charismatic vampire in 18th-century New Orleans. He relates how his respect for the sanctity of human life clashed with his blood-lust, and how the pair came to be adoptive parents to a young girl. Gothic horror based on the novel by Anne Rice, with Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, Kirsten Dunst, Antonio Banderas and Christian Slater

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Education select committee session on the impact of Covid-19 on education with evidence from Professor Russell Viner, President of the Royal College of Paediatrics and Child Health, Dr Jenny Harries, Deputy Chief Medical Officer for England, Osama Rahman, Chief Scientific Adviser at the Department for Education and Dr Dougal Hargreaves, Deputy Chief Scientific Adviser at the Department for Education. from Tuesday 19 January.

programme.title: Select Committees

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Blocks of ice are ordered as the kids attempt to build an igloo. However, the weather is so hot that the frozen cargo melts before it reaches the school

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo is staying at Grandma and Grandpa Moustache’s farm and loves to play with the cat named Cat

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Michael Hill reads An Luch Stèisein, written and illustrated by Meg McLaren and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Documentary about Berta, a feisty young wild boar growing up in the forests of Europe in a family group called a 'sounder'

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: The competition is heating up today - Coinneach Clogaid steals a bicycle and Olaidh Orains meets new friends

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Meanbhag humiliates Mùmantrobha in front of Beadag and he tries to get rid of her – but he regrets this afterwards

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Ina and Jessie are on holiday again and head for the mountains. In their thirst for new experiences they follow a sign that points to an exciting opportunity. However things turn out to be a little bit different from what they were expecting

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Cearcall
programme.desc: A 1980s report from the annual festival in Barra, plus a look at how then-new recording facilities were helping the musicians of Lewis. Presented by Donald Morrison

programme.title: Gàrradh Phàdruig
programme.desc: Spring has arrived and Peter is finally planting out in the garden, while Coinneach helps to paint the walls and the greenhouse starts to fill up with seedlings

programme.title: Around Scotland: Hebrides
programme.desc: From 1970, an insight into life on the islands of Lewis, Uist and Skye from the BBC TV series Around Scotland

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: It is December 1969 and international oil companies are pulling out of Stavanger in Norway after years of test drilling without success. Christian Nyman works as a diver on one of the rigs, while Toril Torstensen's American boyfriend works for Shell, but what happens next affects everyone in town. Starring Anne Regine Ellingsaeter, Amund Harboe, Toril Torstensen and Bart Edwards. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: ALT
programme.desc: Including Kathryn Joseph’s live performance from Glasgow’s QMU, and Justin Currie and Iain Harvey from Del Amitri chat about their hit ‘Nothing Ever Matters’

programme.title: Niteworks: Obair Oidhche
programme.desc: Vicky Kilgour introduces a a showcase concert for the talented band, from Glasgow University. The support artists are the Elephant Sessions and INYAL

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Sheffield, where David Ford comes across a fancy Russian belt, Mark Stevens discovers an ancient book of remedies, and David helps Tim Hogarth to celebrate his birthday

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: President Biden: The Inauguration
programme.desc: Tom Bradby presents an ITV News special from Washington DC as Joe Biden becomes President of the United States

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy refuses to budge on her decision. Cain grows suspicious when cars go missing. Dawn and Will dare to hope they will get Lucas back

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi strikes lucky while digging for dirt on Ray, Sean and Kirk call in their debts from Chesney, and Faye admits to Adam that she attacked him

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter takes a walk through Dartmoor National Park, one of the last great wildernesses in the UK. On a seven-mile circular walk from the iconic Hay Tor, Julia tries her hand at climbing its towering granite rocks, learns about the Dartmoor Pony with naturalist Nick Baker, and discovers the myths and legends of the moor with musician Seth Lakeman

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi confronts Ray with proof of his bribery, Yasmeen suffers a panic attack at work, and Sean encourages Gemma to recruit Vanessa

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: After dealing with the repercussions of her actions from last year, DC Lisa Armstrong is given the opportunity to step up when asked to assist a murder investigation in Morecambe. Tensions remain high as Lisa struggles to do her job whilst following the orders of her previously lower ranking colleague, Med. As the team tries to uncover why the victim was targeted, Lisa and her children are unknowingly stalked by a mysterious figure. Return of the crime drama, starring Morven Christie, Daniel Ryan and Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which three pairs of contestants use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. Looking for the winning words today are folk music loving couple Tim and Kim, mother and daughter Nicola and Tia-Dene, and Gloucestershire couple Dawn and Simon

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up their Thai 'no fish' cakes and demonstrate how to turn meat-free sausages into smoky chorizo for a piri piri traybake. Michelin-starred chef Alexis Gauthier shows how he has transformed a classic French pate into a vegan 'faux gras', there are ideas for healthy smoothies and, for dessert, no-egg mini banoffee meringues with cashew cream and caramel sauce

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his band of dealers head to Newport, south Wales. Karen Dalmeny tries to buy some Arts and Crafts candlesticks, Simon Schneider gets pushed all the way for a gold watch, and funds are needed for a very strange hobby

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as three pairs of contestants call upon their powers of deduction in a battle of words, with the last remaining duo given the chance to double their winnings or leave with nothing. Playing today are police detective couple James and Damien, quiz fanatic Daniel and his partner Sharan and couple Sunth and Sarah

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on a large arcade-style 'coin-pusher' machine. The more counters they collect, the greater the prize fund, with the last one standing competing for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Cally, Robin, Sam and Julie pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain unwittingly causes an accident. Dawn berates Harriet, causing her upset. Tracy thinks she might be in labour

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: From the outset of Covid-19, reporter Paul Brand has followed the stories of those inside Britain's care homes. With all hopes now on the vaccine, Paul investigates the toll of lockdown on residents and carers, meeting families and doctors that fear the isolation, and hearing how the drugs sometimes used to ease anxiety may have stolen away meaningful last days

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Priya is conflicted by Al's declaration. Victoria and Luke matchmake. Amy and Matty seem keen on each other

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: As the nation continues to work through its third national lockdown, Martin Lewis has tips and advice to help people through this tricky financial situation. He also answers viewers' questions on the fourth Self Employed Income Support Scheme (SEISS) grant and furlough payments, and presents the latest News You Can Use and Martin's Big Briefing. With Angellica Bell

programme.title: The Chasers Road Trip: Trains, Brains and Automobiles
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty, Mark Labbett and Shaun Wallace - three of the biggest brains of the quizzing world - travel the world to uncover the cognitive abilities of animals, children and artificial intelligence. They start out in the USA, where they pit their wits against Kanzi, a bonobo ape who is able to communicate with humans using a series of pictures and symbols - and is also thought to be one of the first apes to show an understanding of spoken English. They also meet a chimpanzee memory wizard, a 25-year-old orang-utan that enjoys playing video games and that well-known genius of the ocean, the dolphin

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Late Debate
programme.desc: Simon Harris is joined by a panel of local MPs for a discussion on the latest news and events at Westminster

programme.title: Outbreak: The Virus That Shook the World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the spread of Covid-19 across four continents during 2020. Featuring contributions from medical professionals, a look at the initial outbreak in China and suggestions that the authorities attempted to cover up the danger, and an examination of how the decisions taken by governments and public health officials helped shape the course of the infection

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: For Alice, Harry and their daughter, Charlotte, moving into their new home should have been a dream come true. But it turns into a nightmare when Alice discovers Harry dead at the bottom of the stairs in the property he designed. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The leader of a censorship campaign comes to Oxford on a speaking tour, and Morse is charged with protecting her in light of a series of death threats. A bricklayer working on a controversial pop singer's house is later found dead, and when a second killing occurs, a connection emerges to the speaker's movement, plunging Morse into the middle of a conflict over the nature of British culture

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole reflects upon his time working for the CIA as a person from the past makes a startling reappearance. Murtaugh struggles with his own mistakes after a murder raises questions about a suspect he put away years earlier. Meanwhile, Leo Getz returns as the attorney on the case and tries to convince Trish to join his team. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott, with Thomas Lennon

programme.title: The Cabins
programme.desc: Away from the trappings of modern-day dating, three more 24-hour dates get underway. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie's beloved teddy bear Rupert gets recalled, and he will stop at nothing to get him back. Meanwhile, Peter falls ill and Lois takes his place at a bowling tournament, but he becomes jealous when she gets a little too close to one of his team-mates

programme.desc: Love is well and truly in the air as another set of 24-hour dates begins. Elsewhere, the mood is up and down with an argument threatening to derail an otherwise promising match. A third couple tries their best to get in sync - will they ride off into the sunset?

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Mass immigration from Thrace places the trio in contact with an attractive lodger, and introduces them to an exotic yellow fruit. Meanwhile, Marcus has an unfortunate run-in with an elderly war veteran. Comedy set in Ancient Rome, starring Tom Rosenthal, Joel Fry and Ryan Sampson

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Marcus sees the midnight celebrations at the festival of Saturnalia as the perfect opportunity to make a move on Cynthia, while Grumio goes on a mission to make sure that the sacrificial meat does not go to waste. Comedy, starring Tom Rosenthal, Joel Fry, Ryan Sampson and Sophie Colquhoun

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire finally meets a woman with a sexual appetite as insatiable as his, but things take a turn for the worse when she makes him her sex slave. Meanwhile, a love triangle emerges between Stewie, Brian and Stewie's teddy bear, Rupert

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg accidentally spills her lunch on unstable new pupil Michael, who is nicknamed 'Mental Mike', and must escape his plans to fight her that Friday. Meanwhile, Peter harasses Brian with his nudity, until the dog reaches breaking point

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: After her family accuses her of being a quitter, Francine becomes a bodybuilder. Meanwhile, Klaus lays claim to have invented the high five

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Despite there being a serial killer on the loose, who specifically targets dinner parties, Francine decides to host a dinner party for her closest friends. When the lights go out and guests start disappearing, Roger takes it upon himself to solve the mystery

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: The funniest routines by renowned and up-and-coming comedians - bringing them to life in sketch form, featuring Judi Love, Seann Walsh, Russell Kane, Emmanuel Sonubi and many more

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Sarah Keyworth, Maisie Adam, Russell Kane and Bobby Mair are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Famous faces Laura Whitmore, Louis Smith, Stephanie Pratt, Sair Khan and Ed Gamble take on five friends in a series of challenges. Game show, hosted by Iain Stirling

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Featuring the fastest male to run 50 metres in high heels, the most times that a car has ever been rolled, the record for most rhinestones stuck on someone's body, and the world's shortest cow

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. Andy Murray's mum toughens up her son by using him for target practice, viewers learn what the Mastermind chair does on its day off, and Peter Stringfellow's jumbo 'tablet' gives someone a black eye. The show also features a helicopter for hamsters, a goat who thinks that he is a butler, and Harry is reunited with the daughter that he never knew

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Josh, Scott and Tim go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges hoping to win the affections of Sarah from Lancashire

programme.title: Love Bites

programme.desc: Guest host Tiffany Haddish talks to Jane Fonda about her book, What Can I Do? My Path from Climate Despair to Action

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games

programme.title: Dress to Impress

programme.title: Love Bites
programme.desc: Rather than picking the girls he fancies the most, Robbie picks his date based purely on the food they cook. Will it be love at first bite? Hosted by Scarlett Moffatt

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill takes an alphabetical safari through videos from the animal kingdom. Letters C and D offer plentiful mishaps from domestic situations involving the nation's favourites, cats and dogs - but will he find footage to go with the letter X?

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a selection of comical mishaps filmed by viewers on their webcams, mobile phones and camcorders, featuring a range of celebrity lookalikes, as Simon Cowell takes to the trampoline, Kate Middleton unveils her new workout routine and Ryan Giggs struggles to atone for his mistakes

programme.title: The Cabins
programme.desc: Away from the trappings of modern-day dating, three more 24-hour dates get underway. Nature has brought an end to this year's mating season, but for one lucky dater there's a second chance of love just before The Cabins closes its doors. In the cabin next door, they have a big decision to make before they leave, but nothing a bit of axe throwing won't help them figure out - will the final couple be a match made in heaven?

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins fall victim to Italy's immigration laws after Peter destroys the family's passports during a visit to the country. With the voices of Seth MacFarlane, Alex Borstein and Seth Green

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter, Quagmire and Joe take a trip to Canada on a private jet, which crashes into the wilderness. Peter goes in search of help when Quagmire breaks his legs, but after two months in the wild, he becomes feral and finds it hard to communicate with his family when he finally returns home

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie's beloved teddy bear Rupert gets recalled, and he will stop at nothing to get him back. Meanwhile, Peter falls ill and Lois takes his place at a bowling tournament, but he becomes jealous when she gets a little too close to one of his team-mates

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: While in search of an identity, Jeff starts hanging out with Stan's favourite celebrity chef. Meanwhile, Klaus gets an exciting new outfit

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan finds the CIA Academy cadets the perfect captive audience for his boring stories, and Klaus becomes the hype-man for a famous rapper

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Junior writes a profile of his dad for a school project about his favourite American - but his idealised vision is shattered when Cleveland suffers an embarrassing injury after falling off the toilet. Meanwhile, Rallo is thrilled to discover he is finally tall enough to ride a rollercoaster

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna is unhappy about her relationship with mother Dee Dee, so Cleveland tries to cheer her up by hiring an actress to pose as her mum - but when the real Dee Dee unexpectedly visits, he spots an opportunity for a genuine reconciliation. With the guest voices of Phylicia Rashad (The Cosby Show) and Loretta Devine (Waiting to Exhale)

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Famous faces Megan McKenna, Will Best, Keith Duffy, Ola Jordan and Stephen Bailey take on five friends in a series of challenges. Game show, hosted by Iain Stirling

programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts as Dane Baptiste, Judi Love, Harvey and Max Mills, Chris Taylor and Dawn Ward try to win some normal people £2,500 and some vegan condoms

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy worries that Eric is up to something, and he attempts to bribe a magistrate. Tara carries on as if nothing has happened, leading Biff to worry about her

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An explosion in the woods is linked to the activities of a villain working out of a local quarry. Rachel suspects the owner of supplying dynamite to armed robbery gangs, so Joe goes undercover to infiltrate the workforce - but ends up in mortal danger when his cover is blown. Meanwhile, Peggy sees an opportunity to make money when rumours abound that a meteorite caused the blast. Guest starring Bill Ward and Graeme Garden

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid revolts against the chairman of the local residents association

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' bed collapses and George finds himself short of cash to acquire a new one. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar suspects fishy goings-on after veteran music manager Stan Sparrow chooses to hold his wedding reception at the Aidensfield Arms. When Stan is found dead the next day, investigations reveal his demise may not have been through natural causes. Meanwhile, Geoff is baffled by a boy reporting a crime that has yet to happen, and Peggy's conscience starts to trouble her when she hears the lad has gone missing. Guest starring Sue Jenkins

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric tries to talk Alan into taking on his licence. Tara gets upset after visiting her father's house. Lord Michael attempts to ban Biff from attending Tara's father's funeral

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff does not want to be used by Tara just to shock her friends, and Zak plans to steal some clothes for Mandy's shop from the holiday village

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Les buys an old banger which he intends to do up and sell on. Mavis gets upset on Derek's birthday. Des encourages Samantha to face her parents and tell them what happened

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha stalls on seeing her parents, but Des promises to go with her. Jim is delighted when Steve goes out with Rachel. Fiona takes Maxine with her to find a wedding dress

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A comatose surfer is washed ashore in what seems to have been a freak accident, until sinister facts emerge and Wycliffe is called in. It is not long before the team uncovers a conspiracy - but the case is hindered by a leak to the Press that threatens not only the investigation but also DI Lane's career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. Milner finds his loyalty divided when Foyle is placed under house arrest, but the determined investigator vows to prove his innocence. Drama series, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.desc: Biff and Tara know they are probably the talk of the village. Zak and Butch start a dry cleaning scam at the holiday village. Steve rushes James to hospital

programme.desc: Kim and Steve anxiously wait for news on James at the hospital. Tara has a shock at her father's will reading. Lyn realises that Kirsty is bulimic and offers to get her help

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie is upset when Kevin refuses to let her spend time with his children. Fiona is surprised to see Steve kissing Rachel. Gary worries that Zoe will report him to the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera looks forward to her 40th wedding anniversary. Gary refuses to give Zoe the money up front. Mickey and Pete scare Maud and rob the shop

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish detective's silver wedding celebrations are interrupted by a fresh outbreak of crime on his patch. Five dead bodies have been found on board an abandoned lorry at the ferry terminal and a fugitive is on the run. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Respected surgeon Dan Marsden is shot dead days before his retirement and his teenage daughters Mira and Karen are abducted. Joined by a new addition to the ranks, Vera investigates a mysterious parcel and a connection to the Iraq War as she races against time to catch the killer and save the girls. However, the case soon takes a violent and unexpected turn, striking at the heart of the team. Guest starring Saskia Reeves (Luther) and Dean Andrews (Ashes to Ashes), with Brenda Blethyn, David Leon and Gareth Farr

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Two new residents put PC Mason in an awkward position both professionally and personally and, while the village rallies round to celebrate Blaketon's birthday, he gets more than he expected when Gina goes into labour. David decides to learn to play the piano. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire. Starring Joe McFadden, Tricia Penrose and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kirsty tries to prove to Lyn that nothing is wrong with her. Zak's dry cleaning scam does not go to plan. Will's rugby trial ends in disaster

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Sugdens put Will up while he recovers from his injury. Tara has a shock announcement for Chris and Zoe. Lyn confides in Rachel about Kirsty's bulimia

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen finds Maud helpless on the floor. Jim is hurt when Fiona does not send him a wedding invite. Les turns nasty on Angie for sacking Leanne

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera looks forward to her 40th wedding anniversary. Gary refuses to give Zoe the money up front. Mickey and Pete scare Maud and rob the shop

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. Wendy Craig guest stars, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A murder victim is found in unusual circumstances, buried with a cache of 100-year-old timber. A likely suspect is soon identified, only to be shot dead by a mystery assassin - a development that has repercussions for DI Lane's love life. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour Cornish copper, with Helen Masters

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al discovers that Bert may not be his father and goes in search of the truth. The members of an odd family with poor hygiene and a passion for taxidermy contract salmonella poisoning, but Martin has difficulty persuading them to accept treatment. PC Mylow's bossy sister arrives in Portwenn and sets up a herbalist business in his flat. However, the doctor is forced to take action when her remedies increase his patients' many ailments

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP tries to figure out why one of his patients smells so badly that nobody can bear to be in the same room as him. He also saves a life and a marriage when he realises a local radio presenter's strange behaviour is due to diabetes and not alcoholism. Meanwhile, PC Mylow hopes his luck with the opposite sex will improve when a pretty blonde shows an interest in his driving ability

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid receives a birthday present from the family - but he does not want it. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Les buys an old banger which he intends to do up and sell on. Mavis gets upset on Derek's birthday. Des encourages Samantha to face her parents and tell them what happened

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha stalls on seeing her parents, but Des promises to go with her. Jim is delighted when Steve goes out with Rachel. Fiona takes Maxine with her to find a wedding dress

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A comatose surfer is washed ashore in what seems to have been a freak accident, until sinister facts emerge and Wycliffe is called in. It is not long before the team uncovers a conspiracy - but the case is hindered by a leak to the Press that threatens not only the investigation but also DI Lane's career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. Milner finds his loyalty divided when Foyle is placed under house arrest, but the determined investigator vows to prove his innocence. Drama series, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.desc: Carol asks Joe for assistance when suspicions mount against her following the death of a pensioner who she frequently visited. Peggy is annoyed to find a pile of rubbish has been dumped in her backyard and Gina shares her concerns with Carol as she approaches the end of her pregnancy

programme.desc: Natalie is upset when Kevin refuses to let her spend time with his children. Fiona is surprised to see Steve kissing Rachel. Gary worries that Zoe will report him to the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera looks forward to her 40th wedding anniversary. Gary refuses to give Zoe the money up front. Mickey and Pete scare Maud and rob the shop

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish detective's silver wedding celebrations are interrupted by a fresh outbreak of crime on his patch. Five dead bodies have been found on board an abandoned lorry at the ferry terminal and a fugitive is on the run. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Respected surgeon Dan Marsden is shot dead days before his retirement and his teenage daughters Mira and Karen are abducted. Joined by a new addition to the ranks, Vera investigates a mysterious parcel and a connection to the Iraq War as she races against time to catch the killer and save the girls. However, the case soon takes a violent and unexpected turn, striking at the heart of the team. Guest starring Saskia Reeves (Luther) and Dean Andrews (Ashes to Ashes), with Brenda Blethyn, David Leon and Gareth Farr

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson

programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Two new residents put PC Mason in an awkward position both professionally and personally and, while the village rallies round to celebrate Blaketon's birthday, he gets more than he expected when Gina goes into labour. David decides to learn to play the piano. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire. Starring Joe McFadden, Tricia Penrose and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kirsty tries to prove to Lyn that nothing is wrong with her. Zak's dry cleaning scam does not go to plan. Will's rugby trial ends in disaster

programme.desc: The Sugdens put Will up while he recovers from his injury. Tara has a shock announcement for Chris and Zoe. Lyn confides in Rachel about Kirsty's bulimia

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen finds Maud helpless on the floor. Jim is hurt when Fiona does not send him a wedding invite. Les turns nasty on Angie for sacking Leanne

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera looks forward to her 40th wedding anniversary. Gary refuses to give Zoe the money up front. Mickey and Pete scare Maud and rob the shop

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. Wendy Craig guest stars, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A murder victim is found in unusual circumstances, buried with a cache of 100-year-old timber. A likely suspect is soon identified, only to be shot dead by a mystery assassin - a development that has repercussions for DI Lane's love life. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour Cornish copper, with Helen Masters

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al discovers that Bert may not be his father and goes in search of the truth. The members of an odd family with poor hygiene and a passion for taxidermy contract salmonella poisoning, but Martin has difficulty persuading them to accept treatment. PC Mylow's bossy sister arrives in Portwenn and sets up a herbalist business in his flat. However, the doctor is forced to take action when her remedies increase his patients' many ailments

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP tries to figure out why one of his patients smells so badly that nobody can bear to be in the same room as him. He also saves a life and a marriage when he realises a local radio presenter's strange behaviour is due to diabetes and not alcoholism. Meanwhile, PC Mylow hopes his luck with the opposite sex will improve when a pretty blonde shows an interest in his driving ability

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Mycroft Holmes joins his brother to investigate the murder of a professor's secretary. The only clue is a golden pince-nez found clenched in the hand of the victim. Charles Gray guest stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Spikings finds his life in danger when he investigates the kidnapping of a police informant's son _ and uncovers corruption at the heart of SI-10. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.desc: A series of robberies is committed on affluent couples travelling down a country lane, and it transpires two of the suspected offenders are wealthy themselves. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Peel are on an express train to investigate the disappearance of an agent. However while travelling, the duo get embroiled in a plot on the life of the prime minister. Adventure, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur unwittingly causes a diplomatic incident by trying to strike a deal with the crew of a visiting Russian ship. Guest starring Rula Lenska, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.desc: Henry Cole joins 3,500 other riders on the Bike for Life ride out, and meets bike builder extraordinaire, Alan Milyard, who tells the story of the Triumph Bonneville

programme.desc: Radio 2 DJ Jo Whiley, Benidorm barman Jake Canuso, Law & Order: UK's Georgia Taylor and comedian Marcus Brigstocke compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh asks the questions

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The team travel to a venue in Hungary that has produced carp weighing more than 40kg in the past. However, they quickly discover it shares a common problem with many lakes in this part of the world - there are also hordes of hungry smaller carp under 20lb

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The hunt for red gold heats up and the lobstermen are forced to put everything on the line to stop their businesses from going under. Rookie skipper Bryce needs to catch at least 100kg, while Cracker discovers that his secret fishing spot has been invaded by other boats

programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport. Sue saves a passenger who is a musician from missing his concert. Elsewhere in the airport, there is a serious breach of security. Meanwhile, Ian goes in for a promotion

programme.desc: The second Bournemouth host is aristocratic vet Sir Henry, who plans to charm his way to the prize by hosting an evening of relaxed dining

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Countryside-loving Pippa from Bournemouth hopes to impress her guests by throwing a dinner party with every course inspired by fish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bournemouth, spiritual student Simone hopes to wow her guests with a spicy Bajan menu. She's keen to impress her new friend, posh vet Sir Henry, but she's unsure about Pippa, who doesn't hold back as she probes Simone about her student house

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Bournemouth, flamboyant charity manager Jacqueline hopes that Michelin star-style food will win her the prize. With partridge and black pudding scotch eggs, this could be a culinary night to remember, but will Sir Henry and Simone steal her thunder with an unlikely romance?

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game in which contestants pit their wits against the clock. Prue Leith is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fred and Susan live and work on a farm in Norfolk and are looking for somewhere they can escape to during the quieter winter months. They would like a two-bed property in or near Palma, Majorca's sought-after capital, so Jonnie Irwin lines up five options for their budget of £265,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A Yorkshire glampsite owner diversifies into olive oil farming in Italy, a cook from Birmingham teaches vegan cooking in France, and a couple are determined to rebuild their lives in Spain

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Pastry Day for the second heat of bakers and their last chance to win a place in Finals Week. Ravneet's Technical Challenge takes his audience to Spain where a classic snack is given a Junior Bake Off twist. Then the bakers are tasked with creating a Showstopper in the form of a savoury pie for a celebration, using imagination and technique to create truly unique pastry decorations. However, only one contender can will be awarded star baker and one other must leave the tent. Harry Hill hosts, alongside judges Liam Charles and Ravneet Gill

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Simon Cowell provides the voice of the sharp-tongued administrator of a nursery for gifted children who has the job of deciding whether to accept Maggie. As the possibility of successful admission becomes more likely, Lisa feels her position as the intelligent one in the family is being threatened

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Ste tries to get an apology out of James. Elsewhere, Maxine finds herself suffering through a booze-filled afternoon at The Dog with her mother and Brad

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: In an episode filmed in 2019, Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty are joined by guest Joe Lycett. Jamie teaches Joe to make a traditional wild boar and pappardelle ragu from the restaurant he spent time in while holidaying in Italy, and the comedian wears an authentic Italian housecoat to complete the look. Jamie also celebrates the weekend with a scrumptious veggie tikka masala, with beautiful marinated and grilled veggies served with paneer cheese and amazing little pancakes to mop up the classic sauce. Jimmy has a smoking hot tip for oven-cooked barbecue ribs that looks impressive, but is actually super simple to do

programme.title: It's a Sin
programme.desc: Russell T Davies' drama, set in the 1980s during the Aids crisis, following the lives of a group of young gay men. September 1981 finds 18-year-old Ritchie leaving the Isle of Wight to go to university. In London, Roscoe walks out of parents' home, while Colin arrives from Wales to start a new career on Savile Row. The three men meet, and along with fellow student Ash and best friend Jill form a gang and move into a flat, christened the Pink Palace. However, a distant threat on the horizon means that life in the 1980s might be all too precious. Olly Alexander, Omari Douglas and Callum Scott Howells star

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Socially distanced trio Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by celebrity guests for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A parliamentary assistant fears talking politics scares men away, but may have met her match in an outspoken journalist. A 69-year-old former roadie is looking for someone to settle down with after surviving cancer, while a Lithuanian spa therapist searches for an older man who she can start a family with

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts the show in which potential dates bare all bit by bit in a bid to prove they are the one. First up is 27-year-old flight attendant Alex from Bristol. Having never had a serious relationship, he is hoping to land himself a man for the long haul. He is followed by 26-year-old Laura from Gloucestershire, a horse-loving country girl who is looking for love

programme.title: The Quiet Ones (2014)
programme.desc: An academic scientist recruits a group of Oxford students for a strange experiment, performing a series of tests on a woman in the hope of tapping into her apparent psychic abilities and deliberately manifesting a poltergeist. However, they soon find they are unable to control the dark forces they unleash. Horror, starring Jared Harris and Sam Claflin

programme.title: Back
programme.desc: Return of the comedy series starring David Mitchell and Robert Webb. After a long absence, a rejuvenated Stephen heads back to the John Barleycorn, just as Andrew is starting to feel restricted and weighed down by the mundanities of being a provincial pub landlord. However, the opening of a rival pub just up the road evokes curiosity - especially when it becomes apparent that the new establishment needs a manager

programme.title: The Great
programme.desc: Peter nervously writes a speech for his military and Generals - his father's old buddies. Catherine presents her ideals for a new Russia to Orlo and Marial, who think these radical ideas need to be toned down. Comedy drama, starring Elle Fanning and Nicholas Hoult

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sports fanatic James Cox hosts the first of the week's dinner parties in Northampton, and he is confident that his French-Italian menu will bag him the £1,000 prize. However, some of his guests - Barb Wiles, Donna Harris, Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash - are left confused by his Vichyssoise soup starter, and the tiramisu causes one of them to choke

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Donna Harris hosts the second dinner party in Northampton, and hopes that three courses of pure comfort-food will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. Donna spends almost as much time fixing her make-up as she does preparing the meal, and when guests Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash discover a secret stash of football shirts, they realise there is more to their hostess than meets the eye

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

However, the competitors are unnerved by her interest in extraterrestrials while snooping around her bedroom, and things go from bad to worse when a seafood starter and undercooked chicken result in tears at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Attention-loving businessman and keen cricketer Duncan Fielding takes centre stage in hosting the final dinner party from Northampton. He creates a menu based on the story of his life - which is full of surprises and full of spice - but it proves a challenge for three of his guests, who like their food mild. His main feature is a chicken and reindeer curry, and the table is impressed to discover that he runs a community pub from the bottom of his garden

Codes, cyphers, and crossword puzzles all lend a hand at finding the killer as Tess and Logan O'Connor partner up to try to decipher clues that seem to go back to Second World War Europe. Mystery drama, starring Lacey Chabert and Brennan Elliott

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: A look at the North Yorkshire Moors Railway, following the lives of some of the workers who keep the wheels turning and the passengers happy on one of the world's most popular steam lines. To pull in the punters in, star engine Royal Scot is hired to headline for a week and for general manager Chris it's an expensive gamble as this big and powerful engine was designed to thunder on mainline rails, while the heritage railway has much smaller platforms and bridges. Until Royal Scot arrives and goes through a gauging run on the line, they won't know if she will actually fit the railway

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: This episode follows the intrepid group of men and women who protect the Tower, know as the Beefeaters, revealing how they live and work inside the country's most famous fortress, and what goes on behind the scenes away from the tours and tourists. Throughout the programme, cameras follow the training of a new recruit, and also venture inside the home of a Beefeater within the Tower

programme.title: The Thames: Britain's Great River with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: Tony goes behind the scenes at the iconic Cutty Sark in Greenwich, meeting with the passionate team who care for this historical tourist attraction. Later, Tony visits a metal recycling yard on the banks of the river, before heading out of London to the Hoo peninsula in Kent, where he discovers a rich history of military defence in one small fort

programme.title: Sadie Hartley: The Clues That Caught the Killer
programme.desc: Documentary examining the vital breakthroughs that helped police catch the killers of 60-year-old Sadie Hartley, who was immobilised with a stun gun and stabbed to death in her own home in January 2016. Detectives quickly identified two suspects, but only patient and thorough police work could uncover the evidence to secure a conviction

programme.title: Hunt for the Canal Murderer: John Sweeney
programme.desc: John Sweeney murdered and dismembered the bodies of two ex-girlfriends before dumping them in canals in London and Rotterdam in 1990 and 1992. This programme examines the police investigation that finally brought him to justice after evading capture for years, and reveals how John Sweeney was ultimately undone by his own hand

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Hull Royal Infirmary staff have to deal with abusive patients disrupting care due to Covid restrictions preventing family members from entering the waiting room. programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: A Lilliputian lope round the Inner Hebrides puts Jane in touch with her Scottish roots, as alongside just six other passengers the singer embarks on what is easily her smallest ever cruise. Jane enjoys unseasonably cloudless skies and sparkling seas as the route takes in the puffin-packed Treshnish Islands and Fingal's Cave on Staffa, the mythic rock formation that famously inspired Mendelssohn's Hebrides Overture. The excursion is rounded off with some traditional trappings, from ceilidhs and weaving to haggis-making

programme.title: Secrets of Your Supermarket Food
programme.desc: The pros and cons of going green. Stefan Gates goes on a crash vegan diet for a month, monitoring the effect on his body. Sian Williams visits a food company that specialises in meat replacements, such as jackfruit burgers and vegan pork scratchings. There's also a comparison of vegetarian ready meals and their meat alternatives, and a look at whether it's better to buy fruit and vegetable locally or from imported from abroad

programme.title: Natural History Museum: World of Wonder
programme.desc: World-leading dinosaur expert Susie Maidment is in the museum basement trying to piece together the skeleton of the first Tyrannosaurus rex ever discovered. It's a priceless specimen but somehow the bones have got muddled up with another dinosaur and it's up to Susie to work out if any of the bits are missing. The experts inspect Guy the gorilla, who was a London Zoo favourite for decades and now sits fully preserved in his own glass case, and the museum launches an ambitious project to capture a sample of every living bug in the UK today

programme.title: Robson Green: Walking Coast to Coast
programme.desc: The actor travels from Chollerford to Walltown Crags, and learns more about how the Romans took care of themselves so far from home. He samples natural cuisine and checks out the spa at Chesters Fort, before paying a visit to the latrines of Housesteads Fort, which were once the envy of an empire. He goes on to pit his fishing abilities against an angling rival, gladiator-style, before climbing Steel Rigg to reach the route's highest summit

programme.title: 10 Mistakes That Sank the Titanic
programme.desc: Documentary examining the chain of events that led to the maritime disaster in April 1912, when the ship struck an iceberg in the north Atlantic and went down with around 1,500 passengers and crew. From the procedure for fixing rivets into the ship's bows, the delays in its construction that pushed the maiden voyage into perilous iceberg season, and the astonishing fact that the lookouts on watch that fateful night had no access to binoculars, this film reveals the 10 key mistakes that conspired to sink the supposedly unsinkable Titanic

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: Scientific data and digital re-creations are used to explore the wreckage of ships from the Battle of Jutland in 1916 to try to answer a question that has gone unanswered for more than 100 years - which side actually won. The programme also examines the wrecks at the bottom of the Scapa Flow, where the German fleet was scuttled in 1919

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed
