When she later died under suspicious circumstances, he became convinced she was killed by her jealous sister-in-law, and decided to avenge her death by committing a murder of his own. Drama, starring James Mason, Pamela Kellino and Rosamund John

programme.title: The Chiltern Hundreds (1949)
programme.desc: An aristocrat fails to be elected as a Tory MP, so decides to stand for Labour instead _ only to learn his butler, determined to maintain class distinctions, is running against him. Comedy, starring Cecil Parker, AE Matthews and David Tomlinson

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks above and alongside two large bodies of water - Ladybower Reservoir and Windermere

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: As the destruction caused by Hitler's V1s in London and the south of England reached critical proportions, a new fighter was introduced to challenge the enemy's flying bombs. Wing Commander 'Bee' Beaumont remembers his first encounters with the Tempest, and the Wilkinson family from Croydon share their account of life on the ground with help from their day-to-day diary

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef joins an unusual supper club that meets on an old Tube carriage, and makes sausage-and-mash for some taxi drivers

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: Shaw Somers, Lucy Jones and Dr Giovanni Miletto give advice to a 42-year-old woman who has been eliminating entire food groups from her diet in an effort to combat her traumatising fish odour syndrome. They also help a teenager who worries severe eczema will prevent him fulfilling his dream of becoming an aeroplane cabin crew member. Three hard-drinking students from Leeds put hangover cures to the test, and Dr Pixie McKenna investigates the veracity of health claims made on food packaging

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s. This edition focuses on the story of glam rock. Featuring David Bowie, Alice Cooper, Jethro Tull and Elton John

programme.title: Budding Business
programme.desc: Millions are being made Australia's medicinal marijuana business and entrepreneurs are riding the 'pot stock' boom. But with patients struggling to access cannabis products who is it benefitting?

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: A schoolteacher in a quiet village in Norfolk is torn between life with her boring husband and embarking on an affair with a pop star. Dawn French and Jennifer Saunders star

programme.title: Hand to Mouth
programme.desc: A working class slice of life comedy, about life, love and family on a budget

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: National Treasure
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane stars as Paul Finchley, one half of a much-loved comedy double act, whose life is turned upside down when he is arrested after an accusation of rape that dates back to the 1990s. Paul adamantly denies having even met the woman making the allegation and his loyal wife (Julie Walters) must decide whether to stand by him, while his troubled daughter (Andrea Riseborough) begins to question her relationship with her father

programme.title: Scramble (2017)
programme.desc: A man ends up in over his head in the world of organised crime when his girlfriend is kidnapped by an underground prostitution ring. His attempts to raise the ransom money land him a job delivering dead bodies for a crime syndicate. Crime thriller, starring Brett Newton and Victor Kelso

programme.title: Dagenham ()
programme.desc: A mother's fight against drugs, death and desperation in the deprived town of Dagenham

programme.title: Ultimate Force
programme.desc: Jamie infiltrates a band of anti-capitalist terrorists who are using a state-of-the-art sniper's rifle, but his MI5 contact in the group loses her nerve and reveals his identity during preparations for their next hit. As Box 500 races to crack a coded message giving his location, Henno decides to take matters into his own hands and Red Troop embarks on a tense rescue bid. Drama, starring Jamie Draven and Ross Kemp

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring and revealing Shadwell and Grove Park for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra challenges the monkeys to a race around the island

programme.title: Zot the Dog
programme.desc: The tales of Zot, a good-hearted, fun-loving but rather bouncy dog and his best pal, Clive

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zeek's Animal World
programme.desc: Follow alien robot Zeek as he tries to find out some fascinating facts about our animal kingdom

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: The wacky adventures of the cookie-loving dragons who live in a wizard's castle

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks above and alongside two large bodies of water - Ladybower Reservoir and Windermere

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: The story of the havoc caused by the V1 doodlebugs and the V2 rockets, and of the thousands of deaths that undermined the morale of a war-weary civilian population

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials

programme.title: Budding Business
programme.desc: Millions are being made Australia's medicinal marijuana business and entrepreneurs are riding the 'pot stock' boom. But with patients struggling to access cannabis products who is it benefitting?

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part

programme.title: Forty Thousand Horsemen (1941)
programme.desc: Three Australian volunteers take part in the last cavalry charge in modern warfare while serving in Palestine. First World War drama, starring Grant Taylor and Betty Bryant

programme.title: A Stitch in Time (1963)
programme.desc: A butcher's bumbling assistant causes no end of accidents at work, the latest of which has landed his boss in hospital. While visiting, he meets a little girl who has not spoken since her parents died, and resolves to put a smile on the orphan's face. Slapstick comedy, starring Norman Wisdom, Edward Chapman, Jerry Desmonde, Lucy Appleby and Jill Melford

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's second Vengeance weapon, the deadly V2 rocket, which was responsible for killing 3,000 people in London and the south of England during six terrifying months

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The team offers advice to a 19-year-old fitness instructor who claims to have never eaten a vegetable, and a woman whose menopausal hot flushes are ruining her life. Also examined is a 39-year-old photographer whose diet may be causing her spine to fuse. The results of the first Big Food Trial are announced, and Pixie McKenna investigates the reliability of do-it-yourself food-intolerance tests

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: A cross-border collie suffers a setback when a disc from its spine blows and Noel Fitzpatrick designs a special bolt to substitute it. A four-year-old cat is taken to the surgery after being hit by a car, and it is bad news for the owners when the X-rays reveal its pelvis is badly damaged and the operation may cost thousands of pounds

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours for locals and tourists, exploring Elephant & Castle and Croydon. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: Following the fate of the 'Dunlop' family of foxes in Essex as they fight for survival in the wild. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Comedy series, starring Roger Lloyd Pack and Clive Swift, about two lively pensioners who see their age as no barrier to having fun - particularly when it comes to their attractive neighbour, played by Jane Asher. Written by Sam Bain and Jesse Armstrong, creators of Channel 4's hit sitcom Peep Show, and Simon Blackwell

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Weekend (2016)
programme.desc: Three friends stumble across £100,000, and spend it all on a shopping spree and a wild party. However, thwey then discover that the money belongs to a feared gangster - and he expects them to pay back every penny. Comedy, starring Joivan Wade, Percelle Ascott and Dee Kaate

programme.title: Priest of Love (1981)
programme.desc: Christopher Miles's dramatisation of author DH Lawrence's years in the US, Mexico and England, charting events following the banning of his novel The Rainbow to the writing of Lady Chatterley's Lover. Starring Ian McKellen, Janet Suzman, Ava Gardner, Penelope Keith and John Gielgud

programme.title: Otway: The Movie (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about the punk singer, following his career from the 1970s to the present day and offering his insights into how to survive in the music business

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Notting Hill for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Date with a Dream (1948)
programme.desc: Four wartime performers well known for their rousing concert recitals decide to reform and become a successful nightclub act all over again. Upbeat comedy, starring Wally Patch, Len Lowe, Terry-Thomas, Norman Wisdom and Jean Carson

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series presented by Andrew White, featuring inspirational walks between 2-8 miles from around Britain

programme.desc: Peter Williams investigates the scandal, theft and murder that flourished in the South East during the Second World War, as black market traders and criminals took advantage of Blitz conditions to make an easy living while their compatriots were risking life and limb in defence of the realm

programme.title: Paper Tiger (1975)
programme.desc: An English tutor enthralls a Japanese ambassador's son with improbable fantasies of his heroic exploits, but then finds himself forced to act them out for real when he and the boy are both kidnapped. Comedy adventure, starring David Niven, Toshiro Mifune, Hardy Kruger and Ivan Desny

programme.title: Bloomfield (1970)
programme.desc: An Israeli footballer nearing the end of his career is tempted to accept a bribe to throw his final game. A devoted young fan and a woman who is in love with him prick his conscience, but even if he chooses to do the right thing, his failing skills may not be enough to triumph over younger, fitter opposition. Sporting drama based on Joseph Gross' novel, co-directed by and starring Richard Harris, with Romy Schneider and Kim Burfield

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: The story of the havoc caused by the V1 doodlebugs and the V2 rockets, and of the thousands of deaths that undermined the morale of a war-weary civilian population

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams investigates the scandal, theft and murder that flourished in the South East during the Second World War, as black market traders and criminals took advantage of Blitz conditions to make an easy living while their compatriots were risking life and limb in defence of the realm

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A 47-year-old man receives a shock when consultant surgeon Shaw Somers reveals he is suffering from metabolic syndrome and his body is more like that of a 60-year-old. A 23-year-old woman seeks help with her extremely bad breath, GP Giovanni Miletto puts together an experimental plan to combat chronic fatigue syndrome, and Pixie McKenna discovers how diet can influence people's chances of developing cancer

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: Following the fate of the 'Dunlop' family of foxes in Essex as they fight for survival in the wild. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Harry Patch - The Last Tommy
programme.desc: Harry Patch, Britain's longest-surviving First World War soldier, joins with five other veterans in reflecting on their lives after the 1918 Armistice. Patch, who died in 2009 at the age of 111, recalls the horrors of trench warfare and how he became a passionate advocate for peace in his later years

programme.title: Ultimate Force
programme.desc: Military drama following life for a group of SAS recruits, starring Ross Kemp

programme.title: Desperate Characters (1971)
programme.desc: A burglary turns a middle-aged couple's life together upside down, and makes them question the fundamental values they've always believed in. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Kenneth Mars, Gerald O'Loughlin and Sada Thompson

programme.title: Scramble (2017)
programme.desc: A man ends up in over his head in the world of organised crime when his girlfriend is kidnapped by an underground prostitution ring. His attempts to raise the ransom money land him a job delivering dead bodies for a crime syndicate. Crime thriller, starring Brett Newton and Victor Kelso

programme.title: Felicity (1978)
programme.desc: A teenager in a remote Catholic girls' school seeks solace from her repressive life in reading erotic novels and having a secret love affair with one of her classmates. A trip to Hong Kong organised by her parents opens her eyes to more experiences - both good and bad. Australian drama, starring Glory Annen and Jody Hanson

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours for locals and tourists, exploring Elephant & Castle and Croydon. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The consultants explain how changes in diet could help a 19-year-old dancer deal with Crohn's disease and boost the fertility of a man desperate to become a father. They also help a teenager redefine her relationship with food following gastric bypass surgery. Pixie McKenna examines the effectiveness of energy drinks, and a nationwide survey contributes to research into a natural remedy for insomnia

programme.title: Fight Night International
programme.desc: Carl Froch v Andre Ward. Another chance to see the Super Six final at the Boardwalk Hall in Atlantic City, New Jersey, where the WBC and WBA Super Middleweight titles were also on the line. Nottingham's Froch bounced back from his sole career loss on points to Mikkel Kessler to reach this final by winning decisions against Arthur Abraham and Glen Johnson. However, the Cobra faced a difficult task against the American, who was unbeaten in his 24 professional bouts and won light heavyweight gold at the 2004 Olympics

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Highlights of the third day of the Third Test at Old Trafford, where the series passed the halfway point. England have won the last two Test series between the nations and made the best possible start to this one, with a dramatic 14-run victory at Trent Bridge, followed by an emphatic 347-run win at Lord's. Alastair Cook's men only need a draw to retain the Ashes, while Australia have not claimed the famous urn on English soil since 2001

programme.title: Live Premier League
programme.desc: Liverpool v Burnley (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the top-flight match from Anfield, as Liverpool continue their quest to retain their top-flight title for the first time since the 1982-83 season. The hosts have a strong record against Sean Dyche's team, having only lost twice in 13 fixtures since the turn of the century, and only once in the Premier League during that run.
